outcome_contract.why: The startup loop has no step that requires checking how every screen in the app actually looks when rendered — both in light and dark mode. Issues like invisible text in dark mode, clashing colours, and text that is too small are only caught by doing this audit manually. Adding a screen-by-screen rendered audit to the loop means these problems get found automatically for every product, every time, before launch.
outcome_contract.intended_outcome_statement: The startup loop includes a required rendered UI screen audit at S9B that covers every screen in both light and dark mode, with findings saved as an artifact and blocking issues preventing advance.

engineering_coverage.gap_or_implication: Gate enforces external UI quality; does not change rendered output

engineering_coverage.applicable: 3 block message cases defined in TASK-02 with exact remediation instructions
engineering_coverage.gap_or_implication: Block messages must tell operator exactly what to run

engineering_coverage.applicable: N/A — filesystem-only read; no auth, no untrusted input
Gate result added to advance packet: `ui_sweep_gate`, `ui_sweep_gate_status`, `ui_sweep_report` fields
engineering_coverage.gap_or_implication: Advance packet is the audit trail

engineering_coverage.applicable: 9-case unit tests for gate logic using filesystem fixtures
engineering_coverage.gap_or_implication: Covers: no artifact, wrong business, stale, missing Business field, Routes-Tested=0, Modes-Tested missing dark, S1-Blockers>0, Status=In-progress, clean pass

engineering_coverage.applicable: `report-template.md` gains `Business: <BIZ>` field; gate block/pass packet schema defined
engineering_coverage.gap_or_implication: Template change is prerequisite; existing artifacts block until re-run

engineering_coverage.applicable: Immediate hard gate. Rollback = revert `s9b-gates.md` edits. No data migration.
engineering_coverage.gap_or_implication: Block messages explain remediation for operators encountering the gate for the first time

stage_payload.summary: This plan integrates `tools-ui-contrast-sweep` into the S9B stage of the startup loop by: 1. Adding a `Business: <BIZ>` frontmatter field to the sweep report template (prerequisite for business-scoped gate logic). 2. Implementing `GATE-UI-SWEEP-01` in `s9b-gates.md` — a hard gate that blocks S9B→SIGNALS advance when no recent, rendered, business-scoped sweep artifact exists or when the artifact contains unresolved S1 blockers. 3. Updating `loop-spec.yaml` to spec_version 3.16.0 with the new secondary skill and gate comment block. 4. Aligning documentation in `startup-loop/SKILL.md`, `cmd-advan
goals: Add `tools-ui-contrast-sweep` as a required step in S9B, covering all routes in both light and dark mode.
Add a hard gate `GATE-UI-SWEEP-01` to `s9b-gates.md` blocking S9B→SIGNALS advance when no recent sweep exists or when the artifact has unresolved S1 blockers.
Record this gate in `loop-spec.yaml` with a spec_version bump to 3.16.0.
Update all alignment docs to reflect the new secondary skill and gate.
constraints: Gate must remain filesystem-only at advance time (no HTTP calls).
assumptions: S1 blockers = hard block; S2/S3 = advisory warn only.
selected_approach: Add `Business: <BIZ>` field to sweep report frontmatter template as a required manual field.
Gate reads this field to scope the artifact to the correct business.
Missing or wrong Business field = hard block.
Routes-Tested = 0 = hard block (degraded/non-rendered sweep does not satisfy the requirement).
Immediate hard gate from merge — no grace period.
Why planning is not reopening option selection:
Analysis resolved all three decisions with no remaining operator-only forks.

task_briefs.title: Add `Business:` field to sweep report template
Bump loop-spec.yaml to v3.16.0 and update S9B stage
task_briefs.deliverable: Updated `loop-spec.yaml` with spec_version 3.16.0, S9B secondary skills list updated, GATE-UI-SWEEP-01 comment block added to S9B stage

task_briefs.title: Update alignment docs
task_briefs.deliverable: Updated `startup-loop/SKILL.md`, `cmd-advance.md`, and `tools-ui-contrast-sweep/SKILL.md` to reflect the new secondary skill and gate

task_briefs.title: Add unit tests for GATE-UI-SWEEP-01
task_briefs.deliverable: New self-contained test file `scripts/src/startup-loop/__tests__/s9b-ui-sweep-gate.test.ts` covering 9 gate scenarios; gate-parsing helpers defined inline within the test file (no separate `s9b-gates.ts` module)

areas.label: S9B stage execution
areas.flow: 1. Operator runs `/tools-ui-contrast-sweep`, sets `Business: <BIZ>` in report frontmatter manually. 2. Operator runs `/lp-launch-qa --business <BIZ>`. 3. Operator optionally runs `/lp-design-qa`. 4. Operator runs `startup-loop advance`. Gate checks GATE-LAUNCH-SEC then GATE-UI-SWEEP-01. Both must pass.
areas.seams: Ordering is operator-driven; gate doesn't enforce which step runs first. No CLI flag enforces Business field population — operator must remember.

areas.label: S9B→SIGNALS advance gate
areas.flow: GATE-LAUNCH-SEC runs (unchanged). GATE-UI-SWEEP-01 runs 6 checks: (a) globs artifacts, reads `Business:` field to filter for current BIZ, (b) parses `Audit-Date:` for staleness (≤30 days), (c) checks `Status: Complete`, (d) parses `Routes-Tested` leading integer (must be > 0), (e) checks `Modes-Tested` includes both `light` and `dark`, (f) checks `S1-Blockers = 0`. Pass or block with specific message.
areas.seams: Rollback: revert `s9b-gates.md`. Operator facing a new block must run the sweep with `Business:` field set.

areas.label: Sweep artifact schema
areas.flow: `report-template.md` gains `Business: <BIZ>`. Future sweeps produce artifacts with this field. Gate reads it.
areas.seams: Legacy artifacts (19) lack the field. Any business currently at S9B with an existing sweep must re-run before advancing. Block message explains this.
